rules: Always convert all layers to the same CRS before any spatial operation. Never assume they are already in the same CRS.
When joining tables, ensure the common column data types match — convert both to string if unsure.
GeoPandas does not support on-the-fly reprojection. Always call .to_crs() explicitly before any spatial operation.
After spatial joins, always check for and remove duplicate features using drop_duplicates().
Use make_valid() from shapely.validation to fix invalid geometries before any spatial operation. Do not use geometry.buffer(0) as it can alter geometry shape.
Area calculations in UTM CRS are in square metres — divide by 1,000,000 to get square kilometres.
Never apply the same buffer distance to all water feature types. Rivers, lakes, and drains have different flood extents and need different buffer sizes.
When using gpd.sjoin(), always use the predicate= parameter, never the deprecated op= parameter.
OSMnx features_from_place() returns a MultiIndex DataFrame — always call .reset_index() immediately after.
Never use data.index + 1 to create rank columns — always use range(1, len(data) + 1).
After gpd.sjoin(), always drop the geometry column before any groupby aggregation: joined = joined.drop(columns='geometry'). Failing to do this causes a TypeError on groupby sum.
Never use a 'length' column from road network edges — it may not exist. Always compute edge length from geometry after projecting to a metric CRS: edges['length_m'] = edges.geometry.length
After any analysis, if the result GeoDataFrame has fewer than 3 features, treat it as a Type 2 error — the spatial join or OSMnx query likely failed silently and returned the wrong data.
